# Legal Definition Template for Federal Court Filings (9th Circuit)

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2025-01-01
**Jurisdiction:** United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit
**Citation Style:** The Bluebook (21st ed.)

---

## Template Overview

This template provides a systematic framework for defining legal terms in federal court filings with proper authority hierarchy, citation format, and validation for the Ninth Circuit.

---

## I. PRELIMINARY INFORMATION

**Term to be Defined:** `[INSERT SINGLE WORD OR PHRASE]`

**Case Number:** `[INSERT CASE NUMBER]`

**Court:** United States District Court for the `[INSERT DISTRICT]` District of `[INSERT STATE]`

**Circuit:** Ninth Circuit

**Date of Research:** `[INSERT DATE]`

**Researcher:** `[INSERT NAME/INITIALS]`

---

## II. DEFINITION RESEARCH FRAMEWORK

### A. Authority Hierarchy (Binding in 9th Circuit)

Follow this order of precedence:

1. **Constitutional Provisions** (if applicable)
2. **Federal Statutes** (U.S. Code)
3. **Federal Regulations** (Code of Federal Regulations)
4. **Supreme Court Precedent**
5. **Ninth Circuit Precedent** (binding)
6. **Other Circuit Precedent** (persuasive only)
7. **District Court Precedent** (persuasive only)
8. **Legal Dictionaries** (Black's Law Dictionary)

---

## III. DEFINITION SECTION

### 1. Constitutional Definition (if applicable)

**Citation:** U.S. Const. art. `[#]`, § `[#]`, cl. `[#]` / amend. `[#]`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM QUOTE FROM CONSTITUTIONAL TEXT]"

**Analysis:**
`[Explain constitutional interpretation if necessary, citing Supreme Court cases]`

---

### 2. Federal Statutory Definition

#### A. Primary Statute

**Search Locations:**
- [ ] Title `[#]` U.S.C. (insert relevant title)
- [ ] Other relevant U.S.C. titles

**Citation:** `[Title]` U.S.C. § `[Section]` `([Year of Code Edition])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM STATUTORY TEXT]"

**Legislative History (if necessary):**
`[Cite to committee reports, floor debates, or legislative intent - Pub. L. No. XX-XXX, § X, XXX Stat. XXX (Year)]`

#### B. Related Statutory Provisions

**Citation:** `[Title]` U.S.C. § `[Section]` `([Year])`

**Quoted Text:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM TEXT]"

**Relationship:** `[Explain how this provision clarifies or modifies the primary definition]`

---

### 3. Federal Regulatory Definition

**Search Locations:**
- [ ] Title `[#]` C.F.R. (insert relevant title)
- [ ] Federal Register notices

**Citation:** `[Title]` C.F.R. § `[Section]` `([Year])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM REGULATORY TEXT]"

**Agency Interpretation (if applicable):**
`[Cite to agency guidance, opinions, or interpretive rules]`

---

### 4. Supreme Court Definition

**Primary Case:**

**Full Citation:** *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` U.S. `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([Year])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM QUOTE FROM OPINION]"

**Context:** `[Brief explanation of the case context and holding]`

**Subsequent Treatment:**
- [ ] Still good law (check Shepard's/KeyCite)
- [ ] Overruled/Modified: `[cite case]`
- [ ] Distinguished by: `[cite case]`

#### Additional Supreme Court Cases:

**Citation:** *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` U.S. `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([Year])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM QUOTE]"

---

### 5. Ninth Circuit Definition (Binding Authority)

**Primary Case:**

**Full Citation:** *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` F.`[2d/3d/4th]` `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([9th Cir.] [Year])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM QUOTE FROM OPINION]"

**Holding:** `[Summarize the court's holding on this definition]`

**Subsequent Treatment:**
- [ ] Still good law (Shepardize/KeyCite required)
- [ ] Overruled/Modified: `[cite case]`
- [ ] En banc review: `[cite case]`

**Panel Composition:** `[Judge names - if relevant to analysis]`

#### Additional Ninth Circuit Cases:

1. *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` F.`[2d/3d/4th]` `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([9th Cir.] [Year])`
   > "[Quote]"

2. *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` F.`[2d/3d/4th]` `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([9th Cir.] [Year])`
   > "[Quote]"

---

### 6. Other Circuit Authority (Persuasive Only)

**Note:** These definitions are persuasive but not binding in the Ninth Circuit.

#### Circuit Split Analysis (if applicable):

**Majority Position:** `[Cite circuits following this approach]`
> "[Quote representative case]"

**Minority Position:** `[Cite circuits following different approach]`
> "[Quote representative case]"

**Ninth Circuit Position:** `[Has not addressed / follows majority / follows minority / unique approach]`

#### Representative Cases:

1. *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` F.`[2d/3d/4th]` `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([Xth Cir.] [Year])`
   > "[Quote]"

---

### 7. District Court Authority (Persuasive Only)

**Note:** District court cases within the 9th Circuit are persuasive but not binding.

#### Ninth Circuit District Courts:

1. *`[Case Name]`*, `[Volume]` F. Supp. `[2d/3d]` `[Page]`, `[Pinpoint]` `([D. District] [Year])`
   > "[Quote]"

---

### 8. Black's Law Dictionary

**Edition:** Black's Law Dictionary `[Page]` `([Edition] ed. [Year])`

**Quoted Definition:**
> "[INSERT VERBATIM DEFINITION]"

**Alternative Terms:** `[List related terms if helpful]`

---

## IV. SYNTHESIS AND LEGAL ARGUMENT

### A. Controlling Definition for 9th Circuit

Based on the hierarchy of authority, the controlling definition is:

`[SELECT: Constitutional / Statutory / Regulatory / Supreme Court / 9th Circuit]`

**Controlling Text:**
> "[INSERT CONTROLLING DEFINITION]"

**Citation:** `[Full citation of controlling authority]`

### B. Analysis of Conflicting Definitions (if applicable)

**Conflict Identified:**
`[Describe any conflicts between sources]`

**Resolution:**
`[Apply authority hierarchy and explain which definition controls]`

**Statutory Construction Principles Applied:**
- [ ] Plain meaning rule
- [ ] Legislative intent
- [ ] Canons of construction: `[specify - e.g., ejusdem generis, noscitur a sociis]`
- [ ] Chevron deference (for agency interpretations)
- [ ] Stare decisis

### C. Application to Present Case

**Fact Pattern:**
`[Brief summary of relevant facts]`

**Application:**
`[Explain how the definition applies to your specific facts]`

**Legal Conclusion:**
`[State your conclusion based on the definition]`

---

## V. VALIDATION CHECKLIST

### Citation Verification
- [ ] All citations conform to Bluebook (21st ed.) format
- [ ] All case citations include proper court designation
- [ ] All pinpoint citations are accurate
- [ ] Year of decision included for all cases
- [ ] Year of code edition included for U.S.C. citations
- [ ] Year included for C.F.R. citations

### Authority Verification
- [ ] Shepardized/KeyCited all case law (within 30 days of filing)
- [ ] Verified all cases are still good law
- [ ] Checked for superseding statutes or regulations
- [ ] Confirmed 9th Circuit jurisdiction over cited precedent
- [ ] Verified no en banc or Supreme Court reversal

### Quote Accuracy
- [ ] All quotes are verbatim (no paraphrasing)
- [ ] Ellipses used properly for omissions
- [ ] Brackets used properly for alterations
- [ ] Block quotes used for 50+ words
- [ ] Internal quotation marks and citations preserved

### Substantive Accuracy
- [ ] Definition is on-point for term being defined
- [ ] Context of quoted material accurately represented
- [ ] No cherry-picking or misrepresentation of authority
- [ ] Contrary authority addressed (if exists)
- [ ] Circuit split noted (if applicable)

### 9th Circuit Specific Requirements
- [ ] Local rules compliance verified
- [ ] Standing orders compliance verified
- [ ] Citation to 9th Circuit precedent prioritized
- [ ] Unpublished opinions cited only if allowed (Fed. R. App. P. 32.1)
- [ ] 9th Circuit Model Jury Instructions consulted (if relevant)

---

## VII. FORMAL STATEMENT FOR BRIEF

**Template Language for Court Filing:**

---

### Definition of "`[TERM]`"

The term "`[term]`" is defined by `[controlling authority type]` as follows: "`[controlling definition]`" `[Full citation]`. `[Add additional context or explanation if needed, with supporting citations]`.

`[If statutory:]` This statutory definition is codified at `[citation]` and has been in effect since `[date/year]`. `[If applicable:]` The legislative history indicates that Congress intended `[brief explanation with citation to legislative history]`.

`[If case law:]` The `[Supreme Court / Ninth Circuit]` adopted this definition in *`[case name]`*, `[citation]`, where the court held that `[brief holding]`. This precedent remains good law and is binding in the Ninth Circuit. *See* `[cite Shepard's treatment or subsequent cases affirming]`.

`[If multiple sources agree:]` This definition is consistent across multiple authoritative sources, including `[list sources with short citations]`, demonstrating a well-settled understanding of the term.

`[If there is a circuit split:]` While some circuits have adopted a `[different/narrower/broader]` definition, *see, e.g.*, *`[case]`*, `[citation]`, the Ninth Circuit has adopted the definition stated above. *See* `[9th Cir. case]`.

`[If no binding authority:]` Although no federal statute or Ninth Circuit precedent directly defines "`[term]`," it is commonly understood to mean "`[definition]`" in legal practice. Black's Law Dictionary defines the term as "`[definition]`" `[citation]`, and this definition has been adopted by persuasive authority in `[cite other circuits or district courts]`.

Accordingly, for purposes of this `[motion/brief/argument]`, "`[term]`" should be construed as `[your proposed construction based on the research]`.

---

|---------------|-----------|--------|--------------|
| `[Citation]` | `[Status]` | `[Signal]` | `[Date]` |

---

## IX. PRACTITIONER NOTES

### Best Practices for 9th Circuit

1. **Prioritize 9th Circuit Authority:** Always cite 9th Circuit precedent over other circuits when available.

2. **Published vs. Unpublished:** Under Fed. R. App. P. 32.1, unpublished opinions may be cited, but published opinions carry more weight.

3. **En Banc Decisions:** 9th Circuit en banc decisions take precedence over three-judge panel decisions.

4. **Binding Authority:** Only Supreme Court and 9th Circuit decisions are binding; all other authority is persuasive.

5. **Local Rules:** Always check the local rules for the specific district court (e.g., C.D. Cal., N.D. Cal., etc.).

6. **Standing Orders:** Verify no standing orders conflict with standard practice.

7. **Citation Form:** The 9th Circuit follows Bluebook citation format strictly.

8. **Update Research:** Shepardize/KeyCite within 30 days of filing to ensure continued validity.

### Common Pitfalls to Avoid

- ❌ Citing overruled or abrogated cases
- ❌ Misrepresenting the procedural posture of cited cases
- ❌ Cherry-picking quotes out of context
- ❌ Failing to note circuit splits
- ❌ Ignoring contrary authority
- ❌ Using outdated code editions
- ❌ Improper Bluebook citation format
- ❌ Relying on dicta as holding
- ❌ Confusing standard of review

### Research Efficiency Tips

1. Start with federal statutes and regulations
2. Use headnotes to quickly identify relevant cases
3. Use citator services to find citing references
4. Check for treatises or practice guides on the topic
5. Review 9th Circuit Model Jury Instructions for plain-language definitions
6. Consider analogous terms if direct definition unavailable

---

## X. SIGNATURE BLOCK

**Research Completed By:** `[Name]`

**Date:** `[Date]`

**Verified By:** `[Supervising Attorney Name]` (if applicable)

**Date Verified:** `[Date]`

---

## USAGE INSTRUCTIONS

1. **Copy this template** for each term you need to define
2. **Fill in all bracketed placeholders** `[LIKE THIS]` with actual information
3. **Complete the validation checklist** before finalizing
4. **Check all citations** using Bluebook (21st ed.)
5. **Shepardize/KeyCite all cases** within 30 days of filing
6. **Delete sections** that are not applicable (e.g., if no constitutional definition exists)
7. **Preserve the authority hierarchy** - don't skip to dictionary if statute exists
8. **Save completed template** with filename format: `DEF_[TERM]_[CASE-NUMBER]_[DATE].md`
